mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate compresses equity valuations through a higher discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of non-overlapping periods when the federal funds rate is elevated, or the rank information coefficient falls below 0.03 over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals insufficient labor absorption that weakens household income growth and consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, or the directional accuracy remains below 52 percent over a three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous presence of high consumer price inflation and a restrictive federal funds rate compresses corporate profit margins through elevated input costs and increases equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a rank information coefficient below 0.03 over any two-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of high-inflation high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery, gross domestic product exceeding 23500 billion chained 2017 dollars confirms a resilient demand rebound that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances when GDP exceeds 23500, or the rank information coefficient falls below 0.03 over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 102 signals a rebound in manufacturing activity and capacity utilization that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO exceeds 102, or the directional accuracy falls below 53 percent over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor income and household consumption capacity, which increases aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when payrolls increase, or the rank information coefficient falls below 0.03 over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1200 thousand units signal a durable construction and mortgage credit cycle that supports employment and materials demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances when housing starts exceed 1200, or the rank information coefficient falls below 0.03 over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of falling unemployment alongside rising nonfarm payrolls indicates a tightening labor market that accelerates wage income and consumption expenditures, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS yields a rank information coefficient below 0.03 over any two-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of joint high-payroll low-unemployment periods.
